other_observers: Leon Bright, Bill McGurn, and other Arkansas Valley Audubon members at the "Eagle Day" festival.; I found an adult Mew Gull in October, which is probably this bird. Mark Peterson saw an adult Mew Gull at Pueblo Reservoir on the Pueblo Reservoir CBC on 12/17/2005, and Paul Hurtado saw an adult in January 2006 at Pueblo Reservoir, probably all the
specific_location: West Fisherman's Point at Pueblo Reservoir
description: While looking at gulls off West Fisherman's Point at Pueblo Reservoir around 11am, I spotted an adult basic plumaged Mew Gull. The gull was a little bit smaller than the nearby Ring-billed Gulls. The things I noticed were the darker gray mantle, all thin yellow bill, dark eyes, and in flight, a bigger white spot in the wing tips. The bird had a whitish head, with some brown streaking on neck area, and all white belly and under tail. The bird showed the white cresent, below the mantle, which Ring-billed Gull doesn't have. The bird was an adult bird. I couldn't see leg color, as the bird was mostly on the water swimming with other gulls. The wing tips in flight showed a bigger white area, bigger than in Ring-billed Gulls.Behaviors: Swimming and flying with other gulls at Pueblo Reservoir.Call: nonePlumage: adult basic
